Status Report: 1-Plaintiff Raul Aguilar'S Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition; 2-Defendant John Sebastinelli'S Motion For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Dominic G. Flamiano, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party Raul Aguilar and for Cross-defendant Aguilar Law Office
Joshua Arce, Esq., for Kearny Street Law Offices, Inc.

NO OTHER APPEARANCE WAS STATED. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification:
1-Plaintiff Raul Aguilar's motion to compel attendance at deposition is denied. Plaintiff may re-notice the deposition of defendant consistent with the prior order of March 30, 2007.
MODIFICATION: Deposition on 10/8 59 10/10, 10/23 to 10/25, 9-5 p.m.
Counsel for defendant or counsel for defendant and defendant to review documents before depositions. Plaintiff to pay $2820 to defendant for costs relating to production within 30 days. Plaintiff also ordered to produce inventory of any stored documents.

2-Defendant John Sebastinelli's motion for a protective order is moot in light of the ruling on Plaintiff's motion to compel attendance at deposition. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req. For Production Of Document Set On And Req. For Monetary Sanctions On The Amount To $1500.00 Against Plntf And Its Counsel Of Record
Off Calendar May-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Jeffrey D. Farrow, Esq., appearing for Defendant/moving party: John Sebastinelli
Dominic G. Flamiano, Esq., appearing for plaintiff/opposing party: Raul V. Aguilar

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: (1) Defendant John Sebastinelli's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Document Request Nos. 40, 42, 44, and 57-50.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 41, 43, 35, and 46 as they are irrelevant.
Further production shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. The production shall be subject to an order limiting use and disclosure of the documents to this litigation.
All requests for sanctions are denied.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Req. For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2065.00 Against Plntf And Its Counsel Of Record
Off Calendar May-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Jeffrey D. Farrow, Esq., appearing for Defendant/moving party: John Sebastinelli
Dominic G. Flamiano, Esq., appearing for plaintiff/opposing party: Raul V. Aguilar

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modification: (2) Defendant John Sebastenelli's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, is granted.
As to Nos. 8, 17, 20, 32, and 35, Plaintiff is ordered to respond further, identifying specific Wells Fargo employees or stating that the identity of Wells Fargo Bank employees is unknown.
As to Nos. 37-126, the objections are overruled. Plaintiff is ordered to respond. As to the objection to the interrogatories as the responses might go to financial worth, the objections have no merit as it would appear that Plaintiff's contentions make the information relevant to the allegations being made.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied.
Modification:
Further responses shall be made within 15 days.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
